raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need find all files under /system/folder1, rename replacing 'special' with 'regular'. Use find -depth to rename directories too? Only files? It says all files under, but directories may not contain 'special'. So only files. Use find -type f -name '*special*' -exec rename. Use mv. Use bash command. Provide one command. Use find with -exec sh -c 'mv "$0" "${0//special/regular}"' {} \; . Use -depth? Not needed. Provide single line.
